name: El Salvador (Republic of El Salvador / República de El Salvador)
summary: El Salvador is a Central American civil-law republic whose family-law framework operates under the Family Code 1993 (Código de Familia, Decree 677 of 1993, effective 1 October 1994), drawing on Spanish civil-law substantive heritage with substantial modernisation. Parental authority (autoridad parental) and child custody are governed by Family Code arts. 206-247. The Supreme Court of Justice (Corte Suprema de Justicia) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Chamber (Sala de lo Constitucional) operates within the Supreme Court with constitutional review jurisdiction. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Family Courts (Juzgados de Familia). Psychology profession is regulated through the Junta de Vigilancia de la Profesión de Psicología under the Ministerio de Salud framework. El Salvador is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard codified in Family Code art. 350. El Salvador acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 May 2001.

statutory_framework.title: Family Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Family Code effective 1 October 1994 drawing on Spanish civil-law substantive heritage with substantial modernisation. Arts. 206-247 govern autoridad parental and child custody.

statutory_framework.citation: Law for the Comprehensive Protection of Children and Adolescents (LEPINA) Decree 839 of 2009
statutory_framework.title: LEPINA — Comprehensive Protection of Children and Adolescents
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal LEPINA Law codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle aligned with UNCRC obligations.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Justice (Corte Suprema de Justicia)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Chamber (Sala de lo Constitucional)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Chamber within Supreme Court with constitutional review jurisdiction.

professional_regulators.name: Junta de Vigilancia de la Profesión de Psicología, El Salvador
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulatory board for psychology profession under Ministerio de Salud framework.

anonymisation_convention: Salvadoran family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: El Salvador independence + Central-American-Federation framework
key_developments.description: El Salvador independence 15 September 1821 from Spain as part of Captaincy General of Guatemala. Subsequent Federal Republic of Central America 1823-1841 framework. Foundational Central-American-Federation framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: La Matanza 1932 + Farabundo Martí + indigenous-massacre framework
key_developments.description: La Matanza (the massacre) January 1932 — estimated 10,000-40,000 indigenous Pipil and peasants killed by military government following peasant uprising led by Farabundo Martí. Substantively distinctive Central American 20th-century indigenous-mass-atrocity framework affecting subsequent demographic and family-law-implementation framework.

key_developments.title: Salvadoran Civil War 1979-1992 + FMLN-government conflict
key_developments.description: Salvadoran Civil War 1979-1992 between FMLN guerrilla forces and US-backed government. Substantive 12-year conflict with estimated 75,000 killed substantially affected political-institutional framework. Foundational pre-Peace-Accords framework affecting subsequent democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: El Salvador ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: El Salvador ratified the UNCRC on 10 July 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2009 LEPINA Law substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Chapultepec Peace Accords + post-Civil-War democratic-transition
key_developments.description: Chapultepec Peace Accords signed 16 January 1992 ending Salvadoran Civil War. Substantive democratic-transition framework establishing contemporary constitutional-democratic-trajectory affecting subsequent family-law-modernisation including 1993 Family Code reform.

key_developments.title: Family Code (Decree 677) + post-Peace-Accords-family-law-modernisation
key_developments.description: Federal Family Code enacted Decree 677 of 11 October 1993 effective 1 October 1994 — drawing on Spanish civil-law substantive heritage with substantial modernisation. Arts. 206-247 govern autoridad parental and child custody. Substantively significant post-Peace-Accords family-law-codification framework.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession + earthquake context
key_developments.description: El Salvador acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 May 2001. Substantive 13 January 2001 + 13 February 2001 earthquakes substantially affected demographic and family-law-implementation framework. Subsequent USD-currency adoption 1 January 2001 substantively affecting cross-border-jurisdiction-practice.

key_developments.title: LEPINA — Comprehensive Protection of Children and Adolescents + UNCRC-aligned framework
key_developments.description: Federal Law for the Comprehensive Protection of Children and Adolescents (LEPINA) Decree 839 of 26 March 2009 codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle aligned with UNCRC obligations. Family Code art. 350 + LEPINA framework substantively significant child-protection-framework consolidation within Latin-American UNCRC-comprehensive-children's-protection-code wave.

key_developments.title: Bukele presidency + Bitcoin-Law + mega-prison + state-of-exception framework
key_developments.description: Nayib Bukele elected President 3 February 2019 (Nuevas Ideas). Subsequent Bitcoin-Law 2021 + Centro de Confinamiento del Terrorismo (CECOT) mega-prison + state-of-exception 27 March 2022 (continuing) substantively reshaping political-institutional framework. Substantively distinctive Central-American populist-authoritarian framework affecting family-law-cross-cutting-areas including detained-parent custody contexts.

key_developments.title: State of exception March 2022 + CONNA institutional framework + detained-parent custody contexts
key_developments.description: El Salvador state-of-exception declared 27 March 2022 substantively distinctive Central-American mass-detention framework with 70,000+ detentions through 2022-2024. Substantively significant for detained-parent custody contexts. CONNA (Consejo Nacional de la Niñez y la Adolescencia) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within LEPINA Law + Family Code 1993 framework. Substantively significant Central American institutional context affecting subsequent family-law adjudication.

key_developments.title: Corte Suprema de Justicia + Sala de lo Constitucional — superior-interest-of-the-child substantive register + Bukele re-election
key_developments.description: Corte Suprema de Justicia and Sala de lo Constitucional continue to develop superior-interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Code 1993 arts. 206-247 + LEPINA Law art. 12 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within state-of-exception context. Nayib Bukele re-elected 4 February 2024 in contested constitutional-amendment process. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: El Salvador operates a Spanish-civil-law family-law framework — places El Salvador in the Central American civil-law cluster with Guatemala, Costa Rica, Panama, Honduras, Nicaragua.
LEPINA 2009 is structurally aligned with the broader Latin American UNCRC-influenced comprehensive children's-protection-code wave.
Hague Convention 1980 accession 2001 places El Salvador in the Hague Latin American cluster.
editorial_notes: El Salvador jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full independence-to-Bukele trajectory: 1821-El-Salvador-independence-+-Central-American-Federation + 1932-La-Matanza-+-Farabundo-Martí + 1980-Salvadoran-Civil-War + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1992-Chapultepec-Peace-Accords + 1993-Family-Code-Decree-677 + 2001-Hague-Convention-1980-accession-+-USD-adoption + 2009-LEPINA-Decree-839 + 2019-Bukele-presidency-+-Bitcoin-Law-+-CECOT-+-state-of-exception + 2024-Corte-Suprema-+-Sala-de-lo-Constitucional-superior-interest-+-Bukele-re-election.
Civil-law Central America (Family Code 1993 + LEPINA 2009 + Hague Convention 1980 accession 2001).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive superior-interest-of-the-child analysis under Family Code 1993 + LEPINA 2009 + Hague Convention 1980 framework within state-of-exception context without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Latin-American + civil-law + Central-American + La-Matanza-1932-distinctive-indigenous-massacre + Salvadoran-Civil-War-1979-1992-75000-killed + Chapultepec-Peace-Accords-1992 + USD-currency-adoption-2001-only-El-Salvador-Latin-America-+-Bitcoin-Law-2021 + CECOT-mega-prison + state-of-exception-2022-continuing-distinctive + Bukele-populist-authoritarian-framework + Hague-Convention-1980-accession-2001 clusters within the corpus.
